fr.elements: Main Building; Front Driveway; Landscaped Area; Open Green Space
fr.recommendations: Enhancing the property for emergency response could involve managing the dense vegetation in and around the landscaped area to ensure clear sight lines and facilitate smoother movement of emergency personnel. Creating additional or improved access points at the rear of the main building may enhance accessibility and efficiency in emergency operations. Maintaining open sightlines and easily navigable pathways in the open green space could support responder staging and movement, as well as ensure effective coordination during response activities. Providing clear markings for the main building and differentiating access routes on the front driveway may further improve responder navigation and visibility, thereby assisting emergency personnel in rapidly accessing critical areas of the property.

wildfire.conditions: Scattered Trees; Vegetated Perimeter; Tree Cluster; Defensible Space; Open Terrain; Limited Side Access; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetated Access Route
wildfire.recommendations: Increasing separation between the structure and adjacent vegetation could reduce fuel continuity and enhance defensible space, especially given the presence of vegetation next to the property. Managing the cluster of trees and vegetated perimeter can decrease opportunities for fire to move towards the structure, while supporting suppression efforts. Improved management of the vegetated access route may enhance firefighter access and facilitate emergency response. The presence of open terrain offers an opportunity to maintain or expand this area as a fuel break, further supporting suppression efforts. Although side access is limited, ensuring this area is clear of obstructions and excess vegetation could improve readiness for potential firefighting operations. Consistently managing vegetation concentrations near structures and access routes will contribute to long-term property preparedness and wildfire resilience.
